column1: LIMEN-C-004A\tclaims.md\tEarly 2024 U.S. institutional responses treated AI voice impersonation as consumer-protection, election-misinformation, caller attribution, and telecom-authentication governance questions (context band).\tUpgrade only if a court, regulator-final, or other final-authority event appears for the same family.

LIMEN-C-004B\tclaims.md\tOne shard-007 synthetic-voice cluster supports a bounded rule-anchor to allegation-stage enforcement, including potential USD 1000000 civil penalty and compliance plan terms.\tRecover a direct New Hampshire origin-side authority text or a second non-telecom document-grade row.

Table 1F / LIMEN-VIS-014 bundle\tclaims.md\tShard 007 is publishable as an evidence-architecture panel with explicit role separation: 2 left-anchor rows, 1 governance-context row, 1 default-visible FCC lifecycle row, 1 blocked-origin methods row, 2 multilingual limitations rows, and 1 metadata-only sensitive row.\tPatch if Finland/Lithuania/New Hampshire/Pennsylvania row changes publication role.

Finland / NH / Lithuania / Pennsylvania boundary bundle\tlimitations and promotion-threshold surfaces\tFinland is the best current hidden-to-visible rare-language trigger candidate; New Hampshire is an archive-recovered blocked-origin authority branch with upside; Lithuania is rights-or-access constrained; Pennsylvania is governance-context/metadata-only.\tSmallest move: a compliant Finland direct-article or public-police source; watch for later New Hampshire authority record.
